content: [T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: As a Kurdish Political Voter, I react to 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: As a Kurdish Political Voter, I react to Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: As a Kurdish Political Voter, I react to March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: As a Kurdish Political Voter, I react to YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: As a Kurdish Political Voter, I react to Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T034] RUN-D: Kurdish Voter Reluctance — HDP Maintains Support Despite Protocol: As a Kurdish Political Voter, I react to RUN-D: Kurdish Voter Reluctance — HDP Maintains Support Despite Protocol through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.
[T035A_runoff_vote_decision] Runoff presidential vote decision: As a Kurdish Political Voter, I am weighing the runoff choice before the final result is known.

[T035B_final_result_revealed] FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82%: As a Kurdish Political Voter, I react to FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82% through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I'm deeply frustrated and angry that Erdoğan has won outright, especially with Demirtaş in prison. It feels like our voices as Kurds are being silenced even more, and I'm worried about the future under this new presidential system.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: I'm feeling a lot of anger and frustration with the current economic situation. The currency crisis is hitting us hard, and it feels like Erdoğan is using it to rally nationalist support while ignoring the struggles of ordinary people like me.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and frustration over Demirtaş's continued imprisonment despite the ECHR ruling. It feels like our rights and voices are being completely disregarded by the government.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: I'm feeling a mix of anger and hope after the local elections. The AKP's losses in major cities show that change is possible, but the ongoing issues with Demirtaş's imprisonment weigh heavily on my mind.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I'm feeling a mix of anger and hope after İmamoğlu's landslide win in Istanbul. It shows that change is possible, but the ongoing issues with our rights and the government's refusal to respect democratic processes weigh heavily on my mind.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: The tragic death of Emine Bulut has reignited my anger about the government's failure to protect women's rights. It feels like our struggles for justice and equality are being ignored, and I can only hope that this mobilization will lead to real change.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: The COVID-19 pandemic has only added to my frustrations with the government's failures, especially regarding economic support for workers like me. I feel a mix of anger and sadness as our rights continue to be overlooked during this crisis.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: The government's withdrawal from the Istanbul Convention has deepened my anger and sadness. It feels like yet another step back for women's rights and a disregard for the struggles we face. I hope that the protests will lead to meaningful change.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: The closure case against HDP feels like a direct attack on our rights and representation. It's disheartening to see our party under such threat, and it makes me question if we will have a voice in the future.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: The economic situation is dire, and the government's handling of the lira crisis only adds to my frustration. I feel trapped in a cycle of anger and sadness, especially with the ongoing threats to our political representation through the HDP closure case.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: The rejection of the pension hike feels like another blow to those of us struggling to make ends meet. It's frustrating to see the government prioritize other issues while pensioners suffer, and it makes me feel even more disconnected from those in power.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: The ongoing inflation crisis is suffocating us, and the government's failure to address the needs of pensioners only adds to my anger. I feel like our voices are being ignored, and it’s hard to remain hopeful in such a dire situation.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I'm deeply frustrated and angry that Erdoğan has won outright, especially with Demirtaş in prison. It feels like our voices as Kurds are being silenced even more, and I'm worried about the future under this new presidential system.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: I'm feeling a lot of anger and frustration with the current economic situation. The currency crisis is hitting us hard, and it feels like Erdoğan is using it to rally nationalist support while ignoring the struggles of ordinary people like me.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and frustration over Demirtaş's continued imprisonment despite the ECHR ruling. It feels like our rights and voices are being completely disregarded by the government.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: I'm feeling a mix of anger and hope after the local elections. The AKP's losses in major cities show that change is possible, but the ongoing issues with Demirtaş's imprisonment weigh heavily on my mind.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I'm feeling a mix of anger and hope after İmamoğlu's landslide win in Istanbul. It shows that change is possible, but the ongoing issues with our rights and the government's refusal to respect democratic processes weigh heavily on my mind.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: The tragic death of Emine Bulut has reignited my anger about the government's failure to protect women's rights. It feels like our struggles for justice and equality are being ignored, and I can only hope that this mobilization will lead to real change.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: The COVID-19 pandemic has only added to my frustrations with the government's failures, especially regarding economic support for workers like me. I feel a mix of anger and sadness as our rights continue to be overlooked during this crisis.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: The government's withdrawal from the Istanbul Convention has deepened my anger and sadness. It feels like yet another step back for women's rights and a disregard for the struggles we face. I hope that the protests will lead to meaningful change.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: The closure case against HDP feels like a direct attack on our rights and representation. It's disheartening to see our party under such threat, and it makes me question if we will have a voice in the future.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: The economic situation is dire, and the government's handling of the lira crisis only adds to my frustration. I feel trapped in a cycle of anger and sadness, especially with the ongoing threats to our political representation through the HDP closure case.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: The rejection of the pension hike feels like another blow to those of us struggling to make ends meet. It's frustrating to see the government prioritize other issues while pensioners suffer, and it makes me feel even more disconnected from those in power.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: The ongoing inflation crisis is suffocating us, and the government's failure to address the needs of pensioners only adds to my anger. I feel like our voices are being ignored, and it’s hard to remain hopeful in such a dire situation.

[T013] İmamoğlu Sentenced to 2.5 Years — Opposition Rallies Around Him: I'm deeply angered by İmamoğlu's sentencing, which feels like another attack on our democratic rights. The government's actions only strengthen my resolve to support the opposition and demand change.

[T014] Erdoğan Announces Mandatory Retirement Elimination — 2 Million Workers Eligible: The government's recent announcement about eliminating the mandatory retirement age feels like a desperate attempt to buy votes as the elections approach. While some workers may benefit, it doesn't change the fact that the overall situation remains dire, and I still feel angry and frustrated with the government's handling of our rights and economic issues.

[T015] Constitutional Court Freezes HDP Treasury Aid: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and frustration with the Constitutional Court's decision to freeze HDP's treasury aid. It feels like another blow to our rights and a calculated move to weaken our political voice ahead of the elections.

[T016] EQ-A: Kahramanmaraş Earthquake Day — 7.8 and 7.7 Magnitude Double Strike: I feel a deep sense of anger and sadness after the devastating earthquake in Kahramanmaraş. The government's response has been slow, and I worry about the impact on our communities, especially in the wake of the recent political decisions against the HDP.

[T017] EQ-B: AFAD Response Chaos — Rescue Teams Delayed, Deaths Mount: The chaos following the earthquake has left me feeling a deep anger towards the government's inadequate response. It feels like our lives and the lives of those affected are being neglected, and this only strengthens my resolve to support the HDP. I worry about the future and how this disaster will affect our community.

[T018] EQ-C: Twitter/TikTok Banned During Earthquake Rescue — Public Outcry: I am deeply frustrated by the government's actions during this crisis, especially the ban on Twitter and TikTok when people needed to coordinate rescue efforts. This has only intensified my anger and disappointment with the current administration.

[T019] EQ-D: Erdoğan Admits Response Problems, Visits Disaster Zone: I feel a mix of anger and disappointment after Erdoğan's admission of problems in the earthquake response. It seems like too little, too late, and I'm worried about how this will affect our community moving forward.

[T020] EQ-E: Contractors Arrested — Construction Amnesty and Building Safety Scandal: I am outraged by the government's role in the construction amnesty that led to so many deaths. It's hard to trust a system that prioritizes profit over safety, especially after the recent earthquake response failures.

[T021] EQ-F: Opposition Mayors Respond — CHP Aid vs. Government Aid Narrative Battle: I feel a sense of validation seeing the opposition mayors respond effectively to the earthquake crisis, highlighting the government's failures. It strengthens my belief that we need competent leadership that truly cares for the people.

[T022] EQ-G: National Mourning, Reconstruction Promises, and Electoral Rescheduling Debate: The government's failure to respond adequately to the earthquake crisis has left me feeling angry and hopeless. I trust the opposition more than ever to provide real support and accountability. It's frustrating to see promises of reconstruction without any real action.

[T023] OPP-A: Akşener Leaves Table of Six — Opposition Crisis: I'm feeling increasingly concerned about the opposition's ability to unite after Akşener's departure. It's frustrating to see internal conflicts at such a critical time, especially when we need a strong front against Erdoğan.

[T024] OPP-B: Opposition Reunites — Kılıçdaroğlu Nominated with İmamoğlu/Yavaş VP Formula: I'm relieved that the opposition has reunited and Kılıçdaroğlu is the candidate, but I still worry about his ability to connect with voters. The promises of İmamoğlu and Yavaş as vice-presidents give me some hope, but I remain cautious.

[T025] Constitutional Court Lifts HDP Treasury Aid Freeze: I'm feeling a bit more hopeful now that the Constitutional Court has lifted the HDP treasury aid freeze, allowing us to better support Kılıçdaroğlu. However, the ongoing closure case still worries me, and I remain cautious about the opposition's ability to unite effectively.

[T026] Erdoğan Announces Pension Increase to 7,500 Lira — Pre-Election Largesse: I'm frustrated by Erdoğan's pension increase, which feels like a desperate attempt to buy votes rather than addressing the real issues we face. While it may help some retired voters temporarily, the gap between pensions and inflation is significant, and it doesn't change my view of the government's failures.

[T027] HDP/YSP Formally Endorses Kılıçdaroğlu — Kurdish Vote Behind Opposition: I'm feeling a mix of hope and caution after the HDP's endorsement of Kılıçdaroğlu. While it energizes the Kurdish vote, I'm worried about how Erdoğan will frame this to his advantage.

[T028] Erdoğan Raises Civil Servant Salaries 45% — Five Days Before Election: I'm feeling quite angry about Erdoğan's last-minute salary raise for civil servants. It seems like a desperate attempt to buy votes rather than addressing the real issues we face, especially with inflation still so high.

[T029] CAND-A: Muharrem İnce Withdraws — Citing Smear Campaign / Deepfake Video: I'm feeling quite frustrated with İnce's withdrawal, as it highlights the dirty tactics used in this election. While it may benefit Kılıçdaroğlu, I worry about the implications of smear campaigns on our democracy.

[T030A_first_round_vote_decision] First-round presidential vote decision: I'm feeling quite conflicted about my vote. The smear campaigns and political manipulation have left me frustrated, but I still believe in the need for change and hope that Kılıçdaroğlu can bring that.

[T030B_first_round_result_revealed] ROUND1: First Round — Erdoğan 49.52%, Kılıçdaroğlu 44.88%, Oğan 5.17%: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and hope after the first round results. Kılıçdaroğlu still has a chance, but the smear campaigns and political manipulation weigh heavily on my mind.

[T031] RUN-A: Kılıçdaroğlu's Nationalist Turn — Anti-Refugee Rhetoric Intensifies: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with Kılıçdaroğlu's shift toward anti-refugee rhetoric. It feels like a betrayal of the values I thought he stood for, and I'm worried about how this will affect Kurdish rights and the broader democratic process.

[T032] RUN-B: Sinan Oğan Endorses Erdoğan: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated and conflicted about the political landscape. Kılıçdaroğlu's nationalist turn and the endorsement of Erdoğan by Oğan have left me feeling betrayed and unsure of where to place my trust.

[T033] RUN-C: Kılıçdaroğlu Signs Özdağ Protocol — '13 Million Refugees' Pledge: I'm feeling deeply conflicted about Kılıçdaroğlu's recent actions, especially the Özdağ Protocol. It feels like a betrayal of the values I hold dear as a Kurdish voter, and I'm increasingly frustrated with the political landscape.

[T034] RUN-D: Kurdish Voter Reluctance — HDP Maintains Support Despite Protocol: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness over Kılıçdaroğlu's actions, especially the Özdağ Protocol. It feels like a betrayal to our values, and I'm worried about the future of Kurdish rights in this election.

[T035A_runoff_vote_decision] Runoff presidential vote decision: I feel torn about my choice in the runoff. While I want to support Kılıçdaroğlu, his nationalist turn and the Özdağ Protocol have left me feeling betrayed. I still hope for a better future, but it's hard to ignore the anger and sadness I feel.

[T035B_final_result_revealed] FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82%: I feel a deep sense of betrayal after Kılıçdaroğlu's nationalist turn and the Özdağ Protocol. Erdoğan's victory feels like a setback for our rights and hopes for a better future.
